perturbation.original_text: 1.2 The term "Confidential Information" means all know-how, formulations, recipes, specifications, catalogs, books, price books, maintenance, parts and service manuals, data sheets, sales, service and technical bulletins, customer lists, sales and marketing programs, price lists, cost data, sales aids, such as filmstrips and recordings, and all other publications and information, whether or not reduced to writing, relating to the formulation, manufacture, use, marketing and sale of the AuraGen and/or the Products, as well as any other information which may be divulged by one party under this Agreement to the other in the course of its performance of this Agreement, which is marked as Confidential or which is disclosed under circumstances that reasonably place the recipient on notice of the confidentiality of the information. Confidential Information does not, however, include any information which the recipient can establish (i) was publicly known and made generally available in the public domain prior to the time of disclosure by the discloser; (ii) becomes publicly known and made generally available after disclosure by the discloser to recipient through no fault or breach of recipient; (iii) is already in the possession of recipient without restriction on use or disclosure at the time of disclosure by discloser as shown by recipient's files and records prior to the time of the disclosure; (iv) is obtained by recipient lawfully and without restriction on use or disclosure from a third party without a breach of such third party's obligations of confidentiality; or (v) is independently developed by recipient without use of or reference to discloser's Confidential Information, as shown by recipient's files and records.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 The term "Confidential Information" means all know-how, formulations, recipes, specifications, catalogs, books, price books, maintenance, parts and service manuals, data sheets, sales, service and technical bulletins, customer lists, sales and marketing programs, price lists, cost data, sales aids, such as filmstrips and recordings, and all other publications and information, whether or not reduced to writing, relating to the formulation, manufacture, use, marketing and sale of the AuraGen and/or the Products, as well as any other information which may be divulged by one party under this Agreement to the other in the course of its performance of this Agreement, which is marked as Confidential or which is disclosed under circumstances that reasonably place the recipient on notice of the confidentiality of the information. **However, for the purposes of Article 4.6, Confidential Information shall only refer to data clearly marked as 'Confidential' at the time of disclosure. For all other purposes, the term Confidential Information shall retain its original definition as outlined in the first sentence of this section.** Confidential Information does not, however, include any information which the recipient can establish (i) was publicly known and made generally available in the public domain prior to the time of disclosure by the discloser; (ii) becomes publicly known and made generally available after disclosure by the discloser to recipient through no fault or breach of recipient; (iii) is already in the possession of recipient without restriction on use or disclosure at the time of disclosure by discloser as shown by recipient's files and records prior to the time of the disclosure; (iv) is obtained by recipient lawfully and without restriction on use or disclosure from a third party without a breach of such third party's obligations of confidentiality; or (v) is independently developed by recipient without use of or reference to discloser's Confidential Information, as shown by recipient's files and records.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an ambiguity in the definition of 'Confidential Information'. The original definition is broad, encompassing various types of information. However, for the purpose of Article 4.6 (Confidentiality), the definition is narrowed to only include data 'clearly marked as Confidential'. This means that for the same agreement, something considered confidential under Article 1.2 may not be considered confidential under Article 4.6 unless it is explicitly marked so, creating an in-text contradiction within the agreement on which definition to follow in specific scenarios. This in-text contradiction affects the scope of confidentiality obligations under the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.6 Confidentiality. Each party shall hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence and will in no way disclose or provide such Confidential Information to any person or entity, except (i) with the prior written approval of the owner of such Confidential Information or (ii) as expressly permitted under this Agreement. Each party shall take all necessary measures to prevent such disclosure by its present and future employees, officers, agents, subsidiaries, dealers or consultants during the term of this Agreement and thereafter. Each party shall give prompt notice to the other party of any unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information and must cooperate fully with such other party in remedying each unauthorized use or disclosure. Neither receipt of notice of disclosure under this Section 4.6 nor any action taken by the owner of the improperly disclosed Confidential Information to assist in rectifying an unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information shall constitute a waiver by such party of any breach by the disclosing party of this Article 4. The Parties each expressly acknowledge that all Confidential Information shall remain the sole property of the respective owning party and that all documents and other tangible media that embody any such Confidential Information must be, at such owning party's option, either promptly returned or destroyed, except as otherwise may be required from time to time by applicable law. Confidential Information in documentary or other tangible form, and all copies of it, must be returned promptly to the owning party upon termination of this Agreement or, as applicable, upon earlier expiration of a party's rights thereto in accordance with this Agreement. Each party further acknowledges and agrees that because: (i) an award of money damages in inadequate to compensate a party for any breach of this Article 4; and (ii) any breach of this Article 4 will causes irreparable harm, if there is a breach or threatened breach of this Article 4 by either party, the non-breaching party shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunctive relief and specific performance, without proof of actual damages. The covenants and agreements contained herein with respect to any Confidential Information deemed a trade secret under applicable law shall continue until the information ceases to be a trade secret under applicable law. The obligations with respect to all other Confidential Information shall continue for a period of five (5) years following termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a significant ambiguity and contradiction. While Section 1.2 originally defines 'Confidential Information' broadly, the modified version narrows the definition for the specific purpose of Article 4.6 to only include information 'clearly marked as Confidential'. This directly contradicts the broader definition of Confidential Information, as described in the first sentence of the original section 1.2 and undermines the enforceability of the confidentiality obligations outlined in Article 4.6, because under the modified definition of 1.2, information that is considered Confidential Information in the general agreement may not be regarded as such under Article 4.6 unless it is explicitly marked. This creates a direct in-text contradiction affecting the scope of confidentiality and creating unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: 3.7 End-User Warranty. At all times during the Term of this Agreement, Zanotti shall warrant the Products to purchasers of AETRU Systems in accordance with the terms of its standard warranty attached hereto as Exhibit "C" ("End User Warranty"), as such End User Warranty may be modified from time to time by Zanotti, provided however that at no time shall such End User Warranty provide for a warranty coverage period of less than thirty-six (36) months from the time of purchase by the initial end-user.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.7 End-User Warranty. At all times during the Term of this Agreement, Zanotti shall warrant the Products to purchasers of AETRU Systems in accordance with the terms of its standard warranty attached hereto as Exhibit "C" ("End User Warranty"), as such End User Warranty may be modified from time to time by Zanotti, provided however that at no time shall such End User Warranty provide for a warranty coverage period of less than thirty-six (36) months from the time of purchase by the initial end-user. **Notwithstanding the above, Zanotti reserves the right to offer promotional warranties of shorter durations, not less than twelve (12) months, for specific marketing campaigns, which will be clearly communicated to the end-users at the point of sale.**
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an ambiguity regarding the minimum warranty period. While the initial part of the section stipulates a minimum warranty of thirty-six months, the added clause allows Zanotti to offer shorter promotional warranties (minimum twelve months) for marketing campaigns. This contradiction creates uncertainty about the actual warranty period applicable to end-users, potentially leading to disputes. While section 3.7 does cover the warranty, the introduction of a contraddiction with the section due to the inserted text, creates an in-text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.7 End-User Warranty. At all times during the Term of this Agreement, Zanotti shall warrant the Products to purchasers of AETRU Systems in accordance with the terms of its standard warranty attached hereto as Exhibit "C" ("End User Warranty"), as such End User Warranty may be modified from time to time by Zanotti, provided however that at no time shall such End User Warranty provide for a warranty coverage period of less than thirty-six (36) months from the time of purchase by the initial end-user. Notwithstanding the above, Zanotti reserves the right to offer promotional warranties of shorter durations, not less than twelve (12) months, for specific marketing campaigns, which will be clearly communicated to the end-users at the point of sale.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clause that allows for promotional warranties with a minimum of twelve months, directly contradicting the original clause, as well as the contradicted text, which states that the End User Warranty shall at no time provide for a warranty coverage period of less than thirty-six (36) months from the time of purchase by the initial end-user. This creates an in-text contradiction because both sections are defining the same warranty period with different durations.

perturbation.original_text: 8.6 Applicable Law. This Agreement is deemed made and entered into in the State of California and shall be construed, enforced and performed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without reference, to choice of law. THE RIGHTS AND OBLIGATIONS OF THE PARTIES IN CONNECTION WITH THIS AGREEMENT AND ANY PURCHASE OF THE PRODUCTS SHALL NOT BE GOVERNED BY THE PROVISIONS OF THE 1980 U.N. CONVENTION ON CONTRACTS FOR THE INTERNATIONAL SALE OF GOODS.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.6 Applicable Law. This Agreement is deemed made and entered into in the State of California and shall be construed, enforced and performed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without reference, to choice of law. **However, any disputes related to the End-User Warranty (Exhibit C) shall be governed by the laws of the State of Massachusetts.** THE RIGHTS AND OBLIGATIONS OF THE PARTIES IN CONNECTION WITH THIS AGREEMENT AND ANY PURCHASE OF THE PRODUCTS SHALL NOT BE GOVERNED BY THE PROVISIONS OF THE 1980 U.N. CONVENTION ON CONTRACTS FOR THE INTERNATIONAL SALE OF GOODS.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the applicable law. The original section states that the agreement is governed by California law. However, the addition states that disputes related to the End-User Warranty (Exhibit C) are governed by Massachusetts law. This creates an in-text contradiction, as it is unclear which state's law applies to warranty-related disputes, or other disputes, giving rise to potential legal challenges. While the full agreement is being governed by the state of california law, the contradiction is introduced when determining the applicable law with end-user warrenty, which is now causing an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.6 Applicable Law. This Agreement is deemed made and entered into in the State of California and shall be construed, enforced and performed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without reference, to choice of law. However, any disputes related to the End-User Warranty (Exhibit C) shall be governed by the laws of the State of Massachusetts. THE RIGHTS AND OBLIGATIONS OF THE PARTIES IN CONNECTION WITH THIS AGREEMENT AND ANY PURCHASE OF THE PRODUCTS SHALL NOT BE GOVERNED BY THE PROVISIONS OF THE 1980 U.N. CONVENTION ON CONTRACTS FOR THE INTERNATIONAL SALE OF GOODS.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement shall be construed, enforced, and performed in accordance with the laws of the State of California. The changed text adds an exception stating that any disputes related to the End-User Warranty (Exhibit C) shall be governed by the laws of the State of Massachusetts. This introduces a direct contradiction, as it's unclear which state's law governs warranty-related disputes, creating uncertainty and a potential legal issue, thus adhering to the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.
